jokes.text: D'oh!
jokes.setup: Homer presumably makes some error or has realization
jokes.punchline: His signature frustrated exclamation
jokes.explanation: Homer's signature catchphrase - perfect character integration and economy, culturally iconic

jokes.text: Homer, you silly goose. The weed-whacker's just for the edges. You'll be out here all day.
jokes.setup: Homer is apparently using a weed-whacker to mow his entire lawn
jokes.punchline: Ned's gentle correction reveals Homer's incompetence with yard work
jokes.explanation: Good character-specific humor showing Homer's ineptitude and Ned's condescending helpfulness

jokes.text: put the heat to the meat. Nummy-nummy-num!
jokes.setup: Ned inviting Homer to barbecue
jokes.punchline: Ned's overly cutesy, childish way of describing cooking
jokes.explanation: Classic Ned Flanders speech pattern - overly cheerful and infantile

jokes.text: The Flanders are having a beef-a-thon! Incredible Nedibles! Maudacious vittles!
jokes.setup: Ned describing his barbecue
jokes.punchline: Excessive alliteration and forced puns mixing his and his wife's names with food words
jokes.explanation: Creative wordplay that perfectly captures Ned's try-hard personality and love of forced cheerfulness

jokes.text: Why doesn't he just say so?!
jokes.setup: Ned's overly elaborate barbecue description
jokes.punchline: Homer's frustrated confusion at Ned's linguistic excess
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer reaction - cut through pretense with blunt directness

jokes.text: I don't care if he is the nicest guy in the world. He's a jerk. End of story.
jokes.setup: Marge suggesting Homer give Ned a chance
jokes.punchline: Homer's contradictory statement - admitting Ned is nice while calling him a jerk
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing Homer's irrational hatred despite knowing it's unjustified

jokes.text: Marge Simpson, president of the 'We Love Flanders' fan club
jokes.setup: Marge defending Ned
jokes.punchline: Homer sarcastically creating a fake fan club presidency for Marge
jokes.explanation: Solid sarcastic comeback showing Homer's petty jealousy

jokes.text: Fee, fi, fo, fum! I smell the potatoes au gratin of Marge Simpson
jokes.setup: Ned greeting Marge at the barbecue
jokes.punchline: Mixing fairy tale giant reference with fancy food terminology
jokes.explanation: Creative mixing of children's story with adult sophistication - very Ned

jokes.text: there was some important work at the plant that only he could take care of
jokes.setup: Marge making excuse for Homer's absence
jokes.punchline: Cut to Homer watching Canadian football on TV - the 'important work'
jokes.explanation: Classic setup/payoff structure with visual reveal of Homer's laziness

jokes.text: the Rough Riders who scored only four rouges all last season
jokes.setup: Homer watching Canadian football
jokes.punchline: Obscure Canadian football terminology ('rouges') showing how foreign/boring it is
jokes.explanation: Good cultural satire but somewhat niche - shows Homer choosing obscure TV over social obligations

jokes.text: What if they came back and I was dead from not eating? They'd cry their eyes out
jokes.setup: Homer alone while family is at Ned's barbecue
jokes.punchline: Homer imagining his dramatic death from missing one meal
jokes.explanation: Pure Homer - dramatic self-pity and food obsession combined absurdly

jokes.text: What is it, boy? What? Mmm. Barbecue.
jokes.setup: Dog alerting Homer to something
jokes.punchline: Homer realizes he can smell the barbecue and is immediately interested
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective - Homer's priorities immediately shift to food

jokes.text: Hey, I'm back. Nice seeing you. Hey! Oh! Hey, hey, Homer!
jokes.setup: Homer sneaks over to get barbecue
jokes.punchline: Homer tries to leave immediately after grabbing food, gets caught by enthusiastic Ned
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy showing Homer's selfish grab-and-run attempt

jokes.text: You lie like a fly with a booger in its eye
jokes.setup: Kids playing tag, accusing each other of cheating
jokes.punchline: Bizarre made-up insult with internal rhyme
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully absurd children's insult with creative imagery and rhythm

jokes.text: The fly was funny. The booger was the icing on the cake.
jokes.setup: Kid's elaborate insult
jokes.punchline: Homer analyzing the joke's comedic structure like a critic
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-commentary - Homer unexpectedly showing sophisticated joke analysis skills

jokes.text: 'sinister' being Latin for left-handed
jokes.setup: Ned mentions having a 'sinister motive'
jokes.punchline: Etymological wordplay explaining the connection between left-handedness and evil
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that's both educational and character-appropriate for earnest Ned

jokes.text: That was a joke?
jokes.setup: Ned's etymological pun
jokes.punchline: Homer's confused reaction to Ned's intellectual humor
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer response - completely missing sophisticated humor

jokes.text: Here's the noose I had to wear for ten years
jokes.setup: Ned talking about leaving his pharmaceutical job
jokes.punchline: Calling his necktie a 'noose' - both literally and metaphorically accurate
jokes.explanation: Good metaphor playing on the restrictive nature of business attire

jokes.text: The Leftorium
jokes.setup: Ned announcing his new store concept
jokes.punchline: Portmanteau combining 'left' and 'emporium' - sounds both professional and silly
jokes.explanation: Memorable name that perfectly captures both Ned's earnestness and the absurdity of the concept

jokes.text: It sounds like a pretty dumb idea to me
jokes.setup: Ned excitedly explaining his Leftorium concept
jokes.punchline: Homer's blunt, brutal assessment
jokes.explanation: Characteristically blunt Homer - cuts through Ned's enthusiasm with harsh honesty

jokes.text: Fun? Where is this store, Flanders? The merry old land of Oz?
jokes.setup: Ned saying the store will be fun
jokes.punchline: Homer sarcastically comparing it to a fantasy land
jokes.explanation: Solid sarcastic comeback using cultural reference to mock Ned's optimism

jokes.text: I've got an ambition to do some wishin'
jokes.setup: Ned encouraging Homer to make a wish on the wishbone
jokes.punchline: Homer's forced rhyme showing his attempt to be clever
jokes.explanation: Homer trying to match Ned's verbal playfulness with his own clumsy attempt

jokes.text: Wouldn't it, Flanders?
jokes.setup: Ned saying it would be a shame if Homer's wish doesn't come true
jokes.punchline: Homer's loaded response implying he wished for Ned's failure
jokes.explanation: Perfectly delivered ironic response - Homer's vindictiveness barely concealed

jokes.text: D'oh!
jokes.setup: Homer presumably encounters another mishap
jokes.punchline: Second D'oh of the episode
jokes.explanation: Another perfect deployment of Homer's signature catchphrase

jokes.text: TV gives so much and asks so little. It's a boy's best friend.
jokes.setup: Marge asking Bart about watching too much TV
jokes.punchline: Bart's philosophical defense of television viewing
jokes.explanation: Sharp observation about TV addiction delivered with childlike wisdom

jokes.text: Those children's parents... should be ashamed of themselves.
jokes.setup: Marge criticizes parents who let kids watch too much TV
jokes.punchline: She's literally describing herself and her own parenting
jokes.explanation: Good ironic moment - Marge's lack of self-awareness about her own parenting

jokes.text: I cannot tell a lie. This is a great deal. Hohh!
jokes.setup: Karate instructor doing commercial
jokes.punchline: Mixing George Washington honesty quote with obvious sales pitch
jokes.explanation: Decent parody of cheesy TV commercials mixing historical reference with sales pitch

jokes.text: See, you knock TV, and then it helps you out. I think you owe somebody a little apology.
jokes.setup: Marge complaining about TV, then karate ad provides solution
jokes.punchline: Bart suggesting Marge apologize to the television
jokes.explanation: Smart callback to Bart's earlier TV defense, personifying television humorously

jokes.text: Oh, a little pokey... but things are going to pick up.
jokes.setup: Homer asks how Ned's new store business is doing
jokes.punchline: Ned's forced optimism about clearly failing business
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Ned maintaining optimism in face of obvious failure

jokes.text: Absitively posolutely
jokes.setup: Customer asking Ned to validate parking
jokes.punchline: Ned's made-up enthusiastic response combining 'absolutely' and 'positively'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ned Flanders linguistic invention - overly enthusiastic and slightly annoying

jokes.text: one of the mall security guards took a long look at a left-handed ice cream scoop
jokes.setup: Homer asking about sales
jokes.punchline: Ned's pathetically optimistic spin on having no actual sales
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing Ned's desperate optimism in face of business failure

jokes.text: I already know how not to hit a guy. Can we break out the nunchukus?
jokes.setup: Karate instructor preaching non-violence
jokes.punchline: Bart missing the point entirely, wanting weapons
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bart response - completely missing philosophical point, wanting immediate violence

jokes.text: Pay money to read books. The hell with this.
jokes.setup: Being told to study instead of fight
jokes.punchline: Bart's realization he's paying to do what he hates - reading
jokes.explanation: Great Bart moment - practical kid realizing he's being ripped off

jokes.text: No, I do not know what 'schadenfreude' is. Please tell me because I'm dying to know.
jokes.setup: Lisa mentioning schadenfreude
jokes.punchline: Homer's exaggerated innocent curiosity about a word that perfectly describes his behavior
jokes.explanation: Excellent setup for educational moment - Homer's fake innocence about concept that defines him

jokes.text: Those Germans have a word for everything
jokes.setup: Learning about German compound words
jokes.punchline: Homer's observation about German linguistic thoroughness
jokes.explanation: Good cultural observation that became widely quoted - Homer accidentally insightful

jokes.text: Don't use the Touch of Death on your sister
jokes.setup: Bart pretending to use deadly karate on Lisa
jokes.punchline: Marge's matter-of-fact parenting response to fake martial arts
jokes.explanation: Perfect Marge moment - treating absurd situation with mundane parental authority

jokes.text: We learned how to rip a man's heart out and show it to him before he dies
jokes.setup: Marge asking about karate class
jokes.punchline: Bart's graphic description of violence delivered casually
jokes.explanation: Dark humor delivered with childlike enthusiasm - classic Bart misbehavior

jokes.text: That will learn them
jokes.setup: Bart's violent karate description
jokes.punchline: Marge's grammatically incorrect but enthusiastic response
jokes.explanation: Marge accidentally using incorrect grammar while supporting violence - character inconsistency humor

jokes.text: Left-handed pinking shears. / Pinking shears? / Let's go to the food court and steal... some baked potatoes.
jokes.setup: Kids are discussing what they stole from Ned's store
jokes.punchline: Transition from specific sewing tool to randomly deciding to steal food
jokes.explanation: Mild absurdist humor - random criminal escalation from tools to food

jokes.text: I hear you validate parking tickets without purchase
jokes.setup: Customer at The Leftorium
jokes.punchline: Person only there to get free parking validation, not to buy anything
jokes.explanation: Good example of business failing - people using services without buying

jokes.text: Or as we say around here, 'left as rain'
jokes.setup: Ned saying 'right as rain'
jokes.punchline: Forced modification of common phrase to fit his left-handed theme
jokes.explanation: Classic Ned - forcing his gimmick into common expressions even when it doesn't work

jokes.text: He'd have a great job if he didn't own the place
jokes.setup: Describing Ned's situation at his failing store
jokes.punchline: Homer's accidentally insightful comment about Ned just sitting around
jokes.explanation: Sharp observation about business ownership vs. employment - Homer accidentally wise

jokes.text: Hey, you're that first apple I didn't want. That sinks it.
jokes.setup: Homer dealing with vending machine
jokes.punchline: Personalizing his relationship with vending machine fruit
jokes.explanation: Pure Homer absurdity - treating inanimate objects as personal adversaries

jokes.text: Nobody will take away your precious apples
jokes.setup: Burns completely misunderstanding Homer's complaint about vending machine
jokes.punchline: Burns thinking Homer is possessive about apples rather than complaining
jokes.explanation: Good Burns characterization - completely missing the point with condescending tone

jokes.text: My kingdom for a left-handed can opener!
jokes.setup: Burns struggling with right-handed can opener
jokes.punchline: Paraphrasing Shakespeare's 'My kingdom for a horse' for mundane kitchen problem
jokes.explanation: Excellent cultural reference adaptation - Shakespeare for kitchen appliance frustration

jokes.text: Homer keep all money. Bye-bye, Neddy
jokes.setup: Negotiating with desperate Ned
jokes.punchline: Homer speaking like caveman while being ruthlessly capitalistic
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer - primitive speech patterns while being coldly business-minded

jokes.text: Sounds scrum-diddley-umptious, dear old duddely-doodely
jokes.setup: Ned offering steaks with his made-up words
jokes.punchline: Maude matching his linguistic style with even more elaborate nonsense
jokes.explanation: Great character work showing how Maude enables Ned's linguistic quirks

jokes.text: you scavenger of human misery
jokes.setup: Homer buying Ned's furniture cheaply
jokes.punchline: Marge's surprisingly harsh but accurate assessment
jokes.explanation: Brilliant phrase that perfectly captures Homer's opportunistic nature - became widely quoted

jokes.text: Damned right-handed ledgers. I can't write in these things
jokes.setup: Debt collector explaining billing mistakes
jokes.punchline: Blaming left-handedness for professional incompetence
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist touch - professional making ridiculous excuse for errors

jokes.text: Wait a minute. I'm Homer Simpson. Ned Flanders lives over there. / Oh. / Flanders is in debt? Are you sure? / We don't make mistakes.
jokes.setup: Debt collector mistakes Homer for Ned
jokes.punchline: Homer's shocked delight to learn his successful neighbor is actually in debt
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Homer's schadenfreude at Ned's hidden financial troubles

jokes.text: This is my brother, and he knows karate
jokes.setup: Bullies taking Lisa's saxophone
jokes.punchline: Lisa's threat that sets up Bart's failure to deliver
jokes.explanation: Good setup for Bart's inevitable failure - Lisa's misplaced confidence

jokes.text: It's funny how two wrongs sometimes make a right
jokes.setup: Bart failing at karate but bullies running away anyway
jokes.punchline: Lisa's philosophical observation on the unexpected positive outcome
jokes.explanation: Smart wordplay on common saying that actually applies to the situation

jokes.text: that nice fellow from the bank-- who was only doing his job-- came and locked it up
jokes.setup: Explaining why he's homeless
jokes.punchline: Ned's overly polite description of being foreclosed upon
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ned characterization - maintaining politeness even about his own ruin

jokes.text: I sold it to you for seven cents
jokes.setup: Ned saying even the Bible can't help him now
jokes.punchline: Revelation that he sold his Bible to Homer for almost nothing
jokes.explanation: Excellent reveal showing the depths of Ned's desperation and Homer's opportunism

jokes.text: Kiss me-- I'm left-handed
jokes.setup: Customer in the suddenly busy Leftorium
jokes.punchline: Parody of 'Kiss me-- I'm Irish' buttons
jokes.explanation: Simple parody of cultural reference - functional but not particularly clever

jokes.text: The worm has turned, has it not, my tin-plated friend?
jokes.setup: Burns finally able to open cans easily
jokes.punchline: Addressing the can opener like a defeated enemy with Shakespearean language
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment - Burns treating kitchen appliance as personal nemesis with theatrical flair
